message:
feat(mvcc): propagate cross-process first-committer-wins through group commit + keep volatile EOF lease pages in freelist

Two coupled concurrent-transaction changes that landed together because they
share code paths in the WAL group-commit flow.

1. Cross-process conflict snapshots on TransactionFrameBatch
-----------------------------------------------------------
The connection-local MVCC registry catches stale-write conflicts between
transactions inside one process, but WAL flushers can also receive batches
whose underlying page images were invalidated by commits from another
process. Before this change, those stale batches would simply append a
shadow page image over the peer's work and silently erase committed rows.

Plumb the conflict horizon through the batch:

- fsqlite-wal/group_commit: add `TransactionConflictSnapshot`
  (generation + last_commit_frame + commit_count) and new
  `conflict_pages` / `conflict_snapshot` fields on `TransactionFrameBatch`,
  with a `with_conflict_snapshot(...)` builder.
- fsqlite-wal/lib: re-export the new type.
- fsqlite-pager/traits: add default `WalBackend::conflicting_pages_since_snapshot`
  returning `Ok(Vec::new())` so backends without WAL frame introspection
  are still compatible.
- fsqlite-core/wal_adapter: implement the method. It refreshes the WAL,
  republishes the latest committed snapshot, and if the snapshot has
  advanced (same generation, newer frames) scans WAL frame headers in
  the window `(old_last_commit_frame, new_last_commit_frame]` for page
  numbers that the submitter's batch claims. Generation mismatch is
  treated as "all candidates conflict" (any generation change rewrites
  page histories from our POV). Results are deduped/sorted.
- fsqlite-pager/pager:
  - `predicted_conflict_pages_with_inner` computes the set at Phase A
    (while the staging lock is held), captured into
    `cross_process_conflict_pages`.
  - `attach_group_commit_conflict_metadata` stamps the batch with that
    set plus the WAL pinned read snapshot before queue submission.
  - The WAL append gate now narrows from `Exclusive` to `Reserved` on
    the main-db file, so peer SHARED holders keep running. The stale
    conflict check runs under that Reserved lock and aborts the flush
    with `FrankenError::BusySnapshot { conflicting_pages: "..." }` if
    any candidate page has been committed between the submitter's
    snapshot and the WAL's current horizon.
  - Lock release restored from `Reserved` to `Shared` on exit; error
    messages updated accordingly.
  - Unit test `test_wal_preparation_happens_before_reserved_publish_lock`
    renamed and assertion updated to pin the new
    `LockLevel::Reserved`-during-append contract for bead bd-db300.3.2.
  - All in-file call sites updated to pass an empty `&[]`
    `conflict_pages` slice where the test harness doesn't simulate
    cross-process commits.

2. Volatile EOF lease pages stay in the in-memory freelist
-----------------------------------------------------------
The commit() and commit_concurrent() Phase C1 blocks previously ran
`inner.freelist.retain(|p| p.get() <= effective_db_size)`, dropping any
freelist entry whose page number was past the durable page count.

But the Concurrent-transaction batch allocator advances `next_page` past
those lease pages before they are written; dropping them after commit
creates permanent in-process holes. On a later commit that grows
page_count past the gap, SQLite's consistency check reports the
abandoned pages as "Page N: never used".

Keep the lease entries in the volatile in-memory freelist; they are
already filtered out of `serialize_freelist_to_write_set`, so page 1's
durable freelist_count still reflects only SQLite-compatible freed
pages. New regression test
`test_committed_concurrent_page_lease_pages_are_reused_before_eof_growth`
exercises the batch allocator, commits, and asserts the lowest unused
lease page is reused by the next Concurrent transaction before
`allocate_page` grows the EOF. The older filtering test is renamed
`test_commit_keeps_beyond_db_size_freelist_entries_volatile_only` and
now additionally verifies that page 1's on-disk freelist_count excludes
the volatile-only entry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
